analysis.product_reviewed: Covergirl Outlast Extreme Wear 3 in 1 Foundation

I do feel like this foundation does slightly oxidize... it's definitely setting into like this soft matte finish that I'm really really appreciating... by the end of the day for the person that has very oily skin this is what you'll probably be looking like by the end of the day so definitely will get dewy... I would say for the person who is oily skin that's looking for something to be mad or keep them oil free and look completely over you will have to go in and blot.

five foundations four liquid One Foundation stick ranging between $43 to $57 now they have a variety of different finishes including their OG Dior forever their forever skin glow their forever Hydra nude their backstage face and body and their forever skin perfect Foundation stick so I'm going to zoom you in closer and the First Foundation that we're going to dive into is going to be the OG Dior forever classic Foundation this Foundation is $57 and it's going to give you a matte finish so I would definitely recommend looking into this one if you have oily skin and don't like when your oils come through too much this one will give you maximum wear time now for their claims they say this is a no transfer matte foundation medium to full coverage evens out complexion visibly tightens pores and Smooths the skin and controls shine it also has an 86% based skincare formulation and it does also contain SPF 15 now this is the only one that I have in the shade 2.5 every other shade that I now this one I feel like leaves such a pretty powder soft finish to the skin without it feeling too dry it starts off as a liquid and almost dries down not quite so powdery but it gives an airbrush after all and I feel like none of the dryness is being emphasized and I can speak on this and tell you that this will last all day it will work perfect all day it plays well with other products other primers other concealers other bronzers this is my favorite line of foundation so I don't have anything bad to say so if you need that oil control and you're oily I definitely recommend this but even if you have dry skin and you just want that matte finish maybe for something more long wear in the summer this is fantastic now we're going to take a look at the kind of opposite here the skin glow analysis.product_reviewed: Dior Forever Matte Foundation (newly reformulated)
analysis.key_quote: I really like this foundation I think it worked out very very well on oily skin... it is a very long lasting foundation except for the shade it was my fault I did choose the wrong shade... it didn't break my skin out... I will definitely buy this foundation again.

analysis.product_reviewed: Dior Forever Skin Glow Foundation
analysis.key_quote: I can really see the radiance on my face especially on my cheekbones...it really oxidized on me...I think it's really oxidizing on my face...the foundation is still intact...I'm giving this Foundation a 2.5 stars out of five stars...the reason I'm only giving this at 2.5 stars is because I'm really missing the radiance...I can't find the ingredients...I wouldn't recommend this for an everyday foundation

source.title: Hate The New Estée Lauder Double Wear? These 3 Long-wear Foundations are 10X Better!

transcript: Hello everyone. Welcome. If you are someone who is very disappointed with the newly reformulated Estee Lauder Double Wear foundation, this video is for you. Ever since I uploaded this video sharing my thoughts as someone who has used Estee Lauder Double Wear off and on since 1997, so many people have been asking me to recommend the next best thing, the next best alternative. In case you didn't see that video I uploaded, I did say that I was very disappointed in it, to say the least. I felt like it didn't have the same amount of coverage. I felt that it didn't have the same wear time. And even though I never felt that Estee Lauder Double Wear helped to control my extremely oily skin that well, it did provide some degree of oil control. And so many people in the comments agreed with me. So, in this video, I am ranking three new foundations that I think are excellent alternatives to the Estee Lauder Double Wear. I chose new foundations because so many foundations are being reformulated due to the ingredient that all of them contained that are now banned in the EU. So, even a foundation like YSL All Hours that some of you may have been thinking would be a good alternative. That one has just been discontinued for the same reason. And then hopefully in a few months they will bring out a new one. So, two of the formulas in this ranking are reformulations that have recently launched. And then the third one in this ranking is just a new product all together. So, let's jump right in. My third place alternative is the NARS Natural Matte Longwear foundation. This claims to be a pore-perfecting 24-hour matte foundation with a weightless and breathable serum-like texture, and it offers medium to full coverage. It comes in 40 shades and retails for $55. The shade I'm wearing in this demo is Punjab. Please keep in mind as you're watching these demos that the ring light I'm applying these in front of does alter the appearance of the foundation shade on camera. I'm certain that to a lot of you it probably looks like the absolutely wrong shade for me, but I promise you that once you see it in the natural light, you will agree that it works. All I've put on under all three of these foundations is a moisturizer. I wanted to test them without any primer. It is definitely medium to full coverage. It blends out very easily. It does not emphasize my pores. It does not emphasize any dry patches. I've applied this foundation multiple ways. I've used my fingers, a brush, a sponge, and to be honest with you, all three applications looked really, really great. So, this is what my full face of makeup looked like freshly applied, and this is what I looked like approximately 8 hours later. What surprised me the most about this daily wear test, because I tried a different one of these three foundations every single day and did that 8-hour check-in, I was truly surprised that I'm giving this one a third-place ranking. And that's because last month, I think it was, I had it in my, you know, current favorites or top favorites. In fact, I loved it so much, I went ahead and bought a second shade for when I self-tan. But I have to say this 8-hour check-in surprised me. I was shinier than I recall being any of the other times I have worn this foundation, but then again, when I'm wearing the foundation in my everyday life, when I'm wearing any foundation in my everyday life, I do touch up with powder throughout the day because my skin is that oily. But, for these types of videos, I kind of don't want to do that because I want to see how much oil control these foundations that claim to have oil controlling properties actually hold up to that claim. I was also surprised to see that I had some of the coverage from around my nose disappear. But, that could be because my allergies were really kicking in that day, and I did blow my nose a couple of times. But, that's happened in the past with other long-wearing foundations, and I haven't had that disappearance around the nose. So, I took off some points for that, but I'm not so sure. It may have been a fluke. So, that's why it's still in this ranking. Had it been a total disaster after 8 hours, it would not even be in this ranking whatsoever. I still think it is an excellent option as long as you can find the right shade because even the shades that claim to be neutral do run quite yellow, unfortunately. In second place is the Rare Beauty True to Myself Natural Matte Longwear Foundation. This foundation comes in 48 shades and retails for $38. This claims to be a self-priming and self-setting foundation with breathable medium-to-full coverage and a natural matte finish. It says here that in an independent consumer perception study of 50 people, 94% said it instantly reduced the look of pores, 92% said it instantly blurred skin for soft-focus effect, 92% said it doesn't oxidize, which means the color doesn't change during wear. I was actually sent shades of this foundation. 13, 14, and 15. I was shocked by shade 13 because even though on the Sephora website it says that it is a light neutral {slash} neutral peach, it is extremely peach. You know what? I'm just going to throw in some B-roll of swatches of all three, 13, 14, and 15. 15 ended up being the best for my skin. I know when people are watching a video, they want a simple yes and no, but for me, this isn't simple. It's not a simple review. It's definitely going to come down to your personal preference. They look different on my skin, they perform different, they feel different. This does feel lighter on my skin, it doesn't feel as heavy. My skin feels better after removing it, but I do love the look still of original Double Wear. You have to like a fully matte full coverage look to love original Double Wear, which I personally did. It's a little sad to see, but I would love for them to just keep selling old Double Wear in the old formulation. That would be ideal. Can we sell both? Just new and old Double Wear? I would love that. So that we don't have to stockpile our old Double Wear because this is a brand new foundation, even though it's called Double Wear. There's so many different things about the foundation, and I hope this video highlights that for you and helps you decide whether this is something you want to pick up or not. If you have more of a drier skin type, maybe you want to revisit Double Wear now because at least on my skin, it does go on a lot better for my skin. It's just the wear for me, and there's a total difference in the finish and feel, just so many differences, and I like both. I've gone over all my notes. If there's any question you have or anything that I neglected to address in this video, leave your question in the comment section below, and I will get back to you. Original Double Wear was my perfect filming foundation, and this still looks very glam on camera, which I love. I love a more full coverage foundation just for filming cuz it picks up everything on my skin. So I'm going to still repurchase both formulas as long as I can for the old formulation. But I hope this video helped you decide with the side-by-side, the multiple day wear test, and overall opinion on every single aspect of the new formulation to help you decide whether this is something you want to pick up or not. So thank you so so much for watching, for clicking on this video, for spending some time with me today. I hope this helped you out decide on the newly reformulated Double Wear. Thank you so much for watching, and I will see you guys in my next video.

analysis.product_reviewed: Estée Lauder Double Wear New Reformulation
analysis.key_quote: The new reformulation, although beautiful and actually looks a little bit better on my skin... but for me, this isn't simple. It's not a simple review.

both foundations are considered to be buildable meaning you can build the coverage up overall though after I went in and applied the rest of my makeup and especially set with my powders and then use any sort of makeup sponge to get less coverage if you're looking for Less coverage if you have combination oily and normal skin types and you want something that'll hold up better for you in the is kind of necessary and when you watch makeup artist on the fenty Instagram page apply this foundation they always swipe it on with the brush and then leave it and that is key you cannot go back in and blend this foundation or it'll get patchy and weird you just have to swipe it on in long strokes and then leave it and personally I think this brush that she sells with it is unique and not like any other brush I've seen before because it's like a paddle brush but it's big enough to not leave any streaks I really love it for other foundations too I really truly love this brush and once I started applying the funky foundation with this brush the correct way with the long strokes it was game-changer and I fell in love with the foundation before that I was like this is patchy and terrible and the worst foundation ever why do people like this applied it the correct way in love like I said you can also use a beauty blender and I think it does look good that way too but honestly you're probably gonna save money in the long run by buying the brush and not just losing all of your product and a sponge all right caveat number two to this foundation even if you use a brush you still kind of have to use a lot of product this is not one of those little bit goes a long way like the milk foundation or the Marc Jacobs remarkable I have to use two to three pumps and I'm normally a one to two pumps kind of gal point number three it is imperative that you use some kind of a moisturizing primer with this foundation no matter what your skin type this one will not look good it will not look like butter unless you have primed with something moisturizing I usually only prime with the Smashbox photo finish primer water like like a moisturize and then I prime with the primer water but that's not enough for this one you have to use a moisturizing primer say it with me we have to use a moisturizing primer if you don't it will look dry and it will sink into your pores really weirdly like they will look like little dots it's the weirdest strangest thing Nalla agrees point number four it sinks into my smile lines worse than any other foundation that I own now I've not heard of this being a common problem but I did want to note it because it's happened every single time I've worn it so just know that point number five it dries down quite a bit darker than it is in the bottle so find your shades just like a little bit trickier than usual so just make sure when you're watching them in Sephora if you are that you wait until it has fully dried down before making your shade selection trust me on that one point number six you cannot use cream products over this foundation because it dries to a powder finish I find that cream products are very difficult to blend over top of it again not a huge deal but as someone who enjoys a good cream bronzer I basically just can't use them if I'm wearing this foundation point number seven it's medium coverage now if you're someone with relatively good skin this doesn't apply to you and this might be a pro but I need to note that I do not find this foundation to be buildable like it claims to be because it dries so quickly you cannot blend on top of it or is essentially like putting a cream over a powder it starts to move the foundation underneath and things get patchy and crusty and weird and it's just not pretty and in the application clip you'll see me trying to build it up on one of the sides of my face by working super super quickly like I try and go in before it has dried but it's still even with two or even three layers it doesn't cover all the dark spots so I just I don't think that it can be built to full coverage personally I still wear it and just spot can feel but I just have to note that I don't find it to be buildable to full coverage and caveat number eight it is scented pretty heavily now this could go under a pro or a con depending on if you like the scent but I wanted to but somehow not in a grandma way type of scent but personally no scent at all would be better alright ladies and gents to wrap this all up and then get into the demo and swatches I'll conclude by saying if you do all the right things and apply this foundation properly it is one of the best I have ever used especially for oily skin if you don't you will most likely hate it it's a finicky little halfway there and it seriously does just look like you have amazing skin when you wear this so despite all eight caveats I will still say that this one is a winner in my book I would love to know all your thoughts below in the comment section now let's get into the demo I am wearing shade 130 which is a very yellow shade but I love it it actually does look a little too yellow on camera but that's analysis.product_reviewed: Fenty Beauty Pro Filt'r Soft Matte Long Wear Foundation
analysis.key_quote: If you apply this foundation properly, it just looks like you've got amazing skin... it looks matte but somehow healthy-looking... it dries down quite a bit darker than it is in the bottle... this foundation is one of the best oil controlling foundations I've ever used.

analysis.product_reviewed: Fenty Beauty Pro Filt'r Soft Matte Longwear Foundation
analysis.key_quote: I've had this foundation on for 6 hours or just over 6 hours and I really like it... the makeup has not broken down and it's mainly just shiny on my forehead as well... the rest of my face seems quite good around my nose it hasn't really broken down much at all.

source.title: Armani Luminous Silk Foundation Review ✨ 3 Day Wear Test | acne-prone sensitive, combination skin

transcript:
So my blush is looking super uneven today. Please 
ignore that I'm testing out a new product and it wasn't exactly working out the way that I wanted 
it to. Hey y'all, nice to see you. It's Joanne. If you knew around here, welcome. If you're 
not, welcome back again. Thank you for clicking on another one of my videos. If you've been 
anywhere on beauty TikTok or any beauty spaces on the internet, you've likely already heard of 
the Armani Beauty Luminous Silk Foundation. In this video, I want to really take the time 
to review this product. I want to give it the opportunity to really perform over a 
couple of days just so that I could have a good understanding of how it would perform 
in my everyday life. I will leave all of my complexion match products down below, so 
what shades I am in other foundations in case you're wondering. And I'll also leave 
timestamps down below in the description box. So basically if you have a question, check 
out the description box and if it's not there, just comment down below and I will 
try to respond as soon as I can. The Armani Luminous Silk is a expensive 
foundation. It is $69 for one fluid ounce of this for a reference. I think the majority of 
like high-end foundations are around the $50 mark. So to be $20 more than that is really kind of it. 
It's an outlier. This product actually launched in March of 2000 from what I know now in 2024. 
Um, the most recent expansion of the Shade Range has it standing at 40 shades. So it is quite 
a wide shade range. But in the western space, something that I've seen is, you know, with the 
Fenty initial launch of 40 shades, which was super innovative at the time, I think in 2017, it's been 
six years now and most foundation brands will have a sizable variety of shades. I think 30 is on the 
low end and for the most part the best sellers or the best performing foundations, at least from 
a revenue standpoint will have around 40 to 50 shades. So I would say that Lumina silk is not 
industry leading, it's just the industry standard to have 40 shades. A little bit about me, so I 
have a would say sensitive combination skin and I do have texture from acne scarring. I will break 
out from time to time, but it's never like a giant cystic pimple like I used to have. I went through 
like a round of Accutane and now I'm on like birth control to control hormonal acne. But certain 
products sometimes will trigger a inflammatory reaction in my skin. I also have watery eyes and 
runny and a runny nose from allergies. And this is almost like on a daily basis. I typically wear a 
foundation two to times a week when I go into the office from eight to nine, 8:00 AM to 9:00 PM - 
so over 12 hour wear. This product is called the Armani Beauty Luminous silk perfect low flawless 
oil free foundation. And I got it in Shade 4.5, which is a light to medium with neutral 
undertones. So I did blind by this and I think it kind of leads to a couple problems. So 
because of work from home and generally not having to like leave my apartment as often as when I 
was attending college, I used to be at NC30. I do believe I am a little bit paler and that's like in 
the Mac shades. You can actually get this product in two different sizes. So there is the smaller 
travel size option, which is 0.6 fluid ounces or 18 milliliters and that's $45 US. And then 
there's the full size one, which is 30 milliliters or one fluid ounce and that's $69. So it really 
is better if you get the full size one because you know you're getting almost double the product 
for just 50% more of this travel size price. But I personally went with the travel size one because 
I am not someone who goes through foundation really quickly. Again, if you're wearing it 
only two to three times a week, you're not gonna be using that much foundation. So the key 
details is that this product is a medium coverage, it's natural finish. And I also took a look at the 
ingredients and it's a silicone based foundation. It's also long wearing. So the key considerations 
that I have going into this product is does it oxidize? Does it crease on me? Does it cause dry 
patches, does it stay beautiful? So for example, like you apply it and you know, within the first 
hour it's gorgeous, but then literally it's like just straight downhill from there. Exponentially 
bad, does it get oily and does it transfer? So those are like my key concerns. I know some other 
people might have different concerns and again, you know, I have combination skin, so that's 
not particularly dry and not particularly too oily. And again, I will note that I am not a 
professional makeup artist. The first day I wanted to test out this foundation with no primer, no 
powder, just applied it with a sponge. But the way that they describe it on their website is to just 
use your fingers or a blender. So I think they're really directing those instructions towards people 
who, you know, this is like your everyday woman, you know, she might not have makeup brushes 
or things like that. And so just using your hands using a like a beauty blender or like a 
cheapy sponge. So on this first day I didn't use a primer, I didn't apply a powder or a setting 
spray, I just applied it with a sponge. My initial observations is that there is a fragrance and to 
me it smells like baby powder. I would also say that it's difficult to shade match. I got shade 
4.5 but I think it looks a little bit pink on me, but I saw swatches online and six is really too 
deep or olive. So it's kind of difficult to try to match yourself using the images that they have. 
Perhaps like it's edited in a certain way that it doesn't truly reflect what the actual color is. 
So on the first day using the sponge to apply, I felt like it took a lot of products to build 
up the coverage. For me it took two pumps and I think two pumps on a daily basis will definitely 
eat up the product. However, I like that powder products apply well even without having to set 
the foundation. So it's kind of self setting in a way. Like it's not gonna pull or tug at any 
products that you apply above it. So I like that. I also like that it didn't stick to or create dry 
patches. I will say though, I did test run this product a couple of times before I ever did this 
wear test as well, just so that I felt comfortable using it and that it wasn't any type of user 
error. And I will say that you have to shake this foundation article from the Zoe report from 
I think 2020* noted that the formula is an oil and silicone emulsion, which was kind of an innovation 
at the time of its initial launch in 2000. On the box itself, when you're opening it, there's a 
shaking illustration and that's just to show that you do need to shake this really well before 
use. There were times where it was in the morning, I was running late for work, I needed to like head 
out but my skin was looking awful. So I was like, okay, it's fine. I'm just gonna slap on this 
foundation, I'm gonna use my hands to apply. It should be fine. It was just off - like my skin 
just did not look good like it usually did with this foundation. So I will say that it is a hit or 
miss and so you do have to be a little bit careful with your application. I would also say that 
this foundation does give you that luminosity, but if you're someone like me where you have 
uneven skin texture, where you have perhaps acne scarring or you know, dry rough patches, any kind 
of texture that is not the skin of a baby is going to be highlighted by this foundation at least 
minimally. It's something that only you would really notice. I think if you look in the mirror 
and you're like, oh my god, like did I always have those fine lines? So for me, like my textual 
issues are mainly on my forehead and I noticed that it kind of gave me lines in the light because 
it does give you this slightly, slightly luminous look. And I would, I would kind of describe it 
as like metallic to me because it's got that like reflective aspect to it, but it's nothing 
as intense as a metallic look. And I wore this on a cloudy day, didn't really have any problems. 
I think with cloudy days there's higher humidity. So sometimes certain foundations will break apart 
faster and I would say like the coverage is okay. I don't need a foundation to give me like full 
coverage on application. Like that's not really what I need. I just need my skin to look a little 
bit better, you know, cover up my acne scarring just a little bit and have more even skin tone. 
And I, it did that so, and it didn't get too greasy even without using powder or a primer or 
a setting spray. So on the second day I applied the Armani primer because I had a sample of it. I 
didn't really love it, it was just okay, I didn't felt like it improved the experience. I also felt 
like it was a little greasy, but that could also have been because it was in a packet and perhaps 
the product has separated inside. I applied with a brush. I also applied the Laura Mercier powder. 
So I felt like it mattified really well without getting dry or cakey. Sometimes if a foundation 
doesn't fully set or it doesn't set quickly enough and you're applying translucent setting 
powder on top of it, it kind of gets gloopy and gunky and it didn't do that. And I would say 
that a foundation brush is a higher coverage with just one pump of foundation, but it's still 
medium coverage. So like with a beauty blender it would be like light to medium even with two pumps, 
but a brush would get you two medium with just one pump. But I felt like it was still a very workable 
foundation. On the third day I used primer powder, I applied with a brush and then I also used 
settings berry and I didn't really have any complaints. On the third day I would say the 
glowiness does still show my texture even with a powder, it's like, again, it's not like a dewy 
moisture hydrating type of glow. It's like a very, very, very fine highlighter type of glow. 
Alright, so that, that was the three day wear test. Hopefully that gives you kind of 
a good idea of how the foundation would wear across time. So yeah, now I kind of wanna get 
into my overall thoughts on this product prose. I like that the product was easy to blend and 
that it set down to a satin finish. I like that the product looks skin like it didn't look like a 
mask on my face. I, which is kind of nice 'cause it looks more realistic, you know, like it might 
trick more people into thinking that you don't have foundation or makeup on. I like that the 
product didn't wash off or disappear from my face. 'cause some of the days that I wore this, 
it was a little bit rainy and you know, water might sometimes just like move the foundation 
to places where you don't want it to go. And I also like that it stayed mainly around like on my 
T-zone though I will say that with my runny nose, I definitely had redness here by the end of the 
day, but that's normal. I feel like the products seem true to its word on medium coverage enough 
to let freckles or beauty marks peek through. But it's also adequate coverage for, like for 
me, I do have some like erythema from my acne scarring here. So that's just general redness as 
a splotch and it does help to even that out. I also like that the foundation doesn't feel super 
heavy on the skin. I could feel that it's there. Like I can still feel it right now, but it's not 
enough to feel like my skin couldn't breathe. 'cause sometimes there were foundations that felt 
so heavy I could feel pimples forming because it was just such a bad experience. I don't know 
if that's like, if that's irritation, I if you experience it as a mask like feeling or if it's 
really a mask like feeling, but because of its unb-breathability is a better word because of 
how it makes your skin feel closed off. I will also say that the glass bottle obviously feels 
more premium. You know, it's always really nice to have something high quality, especially 
if you're paying high quality money for it, right? But yeah, those were all of my pros and now 
we get into the cons. Okay, so for me the biggest deter factor is the convoluted shade range. And 
so this kind of ties back to its extensive time on the market. It came out with 24 shades 
and then now it has 40 shades. It's kind of messed up the numbering system in my opinion. 
Oh my god, have I been out of focus this whole time? Hello? I'm gonna cry (T^T). Okay, I think 
I'm in focus. So although there are 40 shades, the shade that I went with using the description 
didn't seem like a match for my skin and it leans slightly pink. This isn't like a full con, right? 
Like if you can find your shade, then that means like this product might actually work for you. 
So I would recommend that if there's a retailer near you that stocks this foundation and you can 
test it, just swing by, check out the shades in person if you can. And again, I'm not knocking the 
foundation for not having my exact shade or making it really difficult for me to find my exact shade. 
If you can find a shade within the range that it's suitable for you or if you've already found it, 
you know, that's perfect, right? Another con is I dislike the powdery fragrance. I would just prefer 
to not have a fragrance in my products. Or if there is just like that regular generic foundation 
smell, which is like low-key paint like, like I prefer that because it just seems more true 
to what the product actually is, which is pigment. I also dislike the look of my forehead scarring 
with this foundation. As you could probably obviously tell, I've said it so many times already 
in this video. I think it just comes down to the fact that luminous products will almost always 
accentuate existing texture. And so I'm not knocking this foundation for that. I'm just saying 
if you are someone who also has texture, perhaps it's something you would wanna avoid. I would 
also say a con here is that skin prep is really important with this product. Otherwise there is 
the possibility of the finished looking really dry and cakey, even though this is a luminous 
foundation with like a natural looking finish, it's actually quite drying on the skin. So it's 
not a hydrating formula. Again, I would say that it's super important to shake this bottle before 
use. You can even see it separating in the bottle. Sometimes there's like a thin layer of oil, like 
very, very thin, but it will mess up with how the product applies to your skin. I would also say 
that the plastic lid and the pump feels so cheap. Like with the Estee Lauder one, you have a metal 
lid, even though there's a plastic on the inside, it's metal at least a little bit so it feels more 
luxe and it's cheaper than this. But this pump, I mean the Estee Lauder one doesn't come with 
a pump, which is always such an L, right? But this pump is super plasticy and I just 
expect more if I'm gonna be spending so much money on this. Overall I would rate this 
foundation a seven outta 10. I would say anything above an eight outta 10 would be 
a repurchase for me. I would not actually repurchase this product and it's due to all 
of the different cons that I listed before. I feel like I would want a product to have barely 
any cons for me to actually repurchase it. And so, yeah, I don't think this is the perfect 
foundation for me. And so that's why I will not be repurchasing it. Oh, one last thing. 
Okay. So I would definitely recommend signing up for the Armani beauty.com website, like 
the brand website for this product because even though it is like super expensive on 
Sephora, you can commonly find this for like 20 to 30% off on the brand website whenever 
there's like a huge holiday. So you know, my motto has always been never pay full price. That money saving grandma 
in me is always looking for a good deal. And so I actually, even though this one I did not purchase, 
at a discount, I basically - I did a promo where I got this and the Power Fabric foundation, which 
I will posting my full review soon. Um, and with that together I got the Armani Code Parfum, which 
I gifted to my boyfriend. I got that completely for free. So I think that that's an amazing deal. 
There's different promotions throughout the year. Okay. Alright, so that is everything. Thank you 
guys so much for listening. It is literally 12AM like my plant lights kept turning off because of 
their automatic timer. But thank you so much for watching this video if you're still here at the 
end. Yeah, I, I really appreciate it. If you guys have any questions, requests, concerns, please 
comment down below. Yeah, so without further ado, thank you guys so much for watching and I 
will see you guys in my next video. Bye.

source.title: L'Oréal Infallible 32 Hour Fresh Wear Foundation Full Day Wear Test on oily skin | Doctors Review

transcript: [Music] i haven't done many foundation reviews lately mainly because i haven't worn any foundation for most of the last two years anyway i still don't wear it daily but there have been rare occasions and for them i wanted something else than the full coverage one that i use for filming something less heavy and the l'oreal unfoldable 32 hours fresh wear claiming a fresh breathable and lightweight formula seem to be a good one to ease my way back in but is it true to its claims and how does it wear on my sometimes oily acne prone and mature skin let's see if you're new you're welcome i'm dr al a physician passionate about skincare and well aging our breakthrough longwear foundation offers up to 32 hours of wear with a weightless formula that allows the skin to breathe as you would expect from the description is a very runny formula that i prefer to either apply with a beauty blender or my fingers old school i know but i found that if i use the brush the flatter ones work the best the fluffier ones i used sometimes left it looking a little patchy or made it emphasize my pores the finish is skin like neither matte nor extra dewy and the coverage is medium yet buildable to full as you can see the pigmentation on my lower cheeks is still shining through as is some hyperpigmentation on my temples but it did a pretty good job at evening up the rest of my face and that was just one layer dab on with a damp beauty blender while it doesn't feel heavy i can definitely feel i'm wearing something similar to how a heavier moisturizer would feel i've worn more lightweight foundations yet definitely heavier foundations as well and it isn't uncomfortable that something on my skin feeling will disappear within the first hour i need to preface that by saying that i did not use a primer or makeup fixing spray when filming these clips mainly because i don't use them when i wear foundation throughout the day if you use one or both you might be able to increase the longevity on your skin the first thing that i notice at around the two hour mark is that it gets a little more dewy around the nose and chain area than i prefer but otherwise i'm still happy with how it looks if you'd went for the review i would powder now but i didn't do it to show you how it performs on its own if i had very dry and flaky bits of skin like the leftovers of a dried up seed or similar the foundation would stick to that by now and emphasize the flax a little now remember that the footage you are looking at here is filmed in natural light and in 4k what you see here is pretty much what you would see when you would meet me in real life from a distance it still looks pretty good but close up you can see it start to separate and disappear a little around the nose with some of the redness i have there shining through other than that though as you can see in the close-up it isn't settling into my lines pores or doing other weird things the foundation disappearing is now visible from the distance around the nose and chin and even more so close up there is still some coverage going on but you can see more texture of pigmentation shining through if i had an important meeting or date but i would touch up now now six hours don't even come close to a workday for the majority of us and i would expect a foundation that claims to be long wearing to last longer than that it is long enough for a night out though which is when i wear foundation and of course depends on your skin and additional preparation steps you took this is right before i take it off apologies for the changing lighting i was using natural light from a north facing window and this one is more flattering than the other clips before the coverage now is almost entirely gone you get a good look at the angry red seat on my chin that previously stayed covered pretty well it looks similar to how my face looked before i put on foundation in the morning with maybe a little less prominent pigmentation on the cheeks but that might be the changed lighting more than the foundation though still it doesn't emphasize my lines or makes my face look worse than i did there don't laugh that happened to me with other foundations so i don't mind it too much i have not obviously tested it for 32 hours but as it claims up 2 which is a sneaky way of saying anything between 1 and 32 hours as possible i didn't expect it to last that long anyway still 6 hours is a little shorter than i would have hoped not shown here is how well it works for touch ups my preferred way is to go in with a damp clean beauty blender to redistribute it and then maybe add initial very lightly in areas where it has completely wrapped off it isn't perfect but looks well enough to see me through the final meeting of the day without having to redo my full face i do enjoy it on skin like mine which can be oily the t-cell was also dehydrated or dry in other areas and that is prone to breakouts as it doesn't emphasize fine lines and wrinkles i think it will work well on more mature skins as well the only skin type i would not recommend this to is very oily ones as my natural oils did start to break it up at around four hour marks which makes me doubt that it will stay long on these skins if you have a lot of texture in dry skin i don't think it would be the best either you will probably need something more hydrating than that have you tried the foundation please tell me your thoughts in the comments below i will link to more videos you might find interesting on the screen and add links to my instagram blog and patreon account in the description box see you soon bye

analysis.product_reviewed: L'Oréal Infallible 32 Hour Fresh Wear Foundation
analysis.key_quote: the only skin type i would not recommend this to is very oily ones as my natural oils did start to break it up at around four hour marks

analysis.product_reviewed: L'Oreal Infallible 24-hour Fresh Wear and L'Oreal Pro Matte foundations

persona.shade_used: 485 Golden Sun for Fresh Wear, 107 Fresh Beige for Pro Matte

analysis.key_quote: 10 hour mark... it's holding up extremely well ... no breakage, no separation on the skin ... both foundations are very good and durable ... if I have to choose one, I'd go with the 24-hour Fresh Wear because it has sunscreen and a great shade range.

purchased my first MAC lipstick and then it was like it just spiraled out of control from there let me know down below if you also started with Mac I feel like a lot of people did this Foundation retails for $33 which is a lot cheaper or somewhat cheaper anyways than a lot of the foundation that are at Sephora slightly more expensive than what you get at the drugstore but drugstore prices are getting up there something that I love about Mac is they've got a gigantic shade ranges for their foundations so this one comes in 42 different shades which is absolutely amazing I wear ncy 42 in this I love their Studio fix powder foundation and I wear an nc43 in that I'm a little more yellow toned so I go towards the NC foundations if you're more pink toned then you would probably lean towards an NW so this as I mentioned retails for $33 you get a fluid ounce in here and it comes in a glass bottle which is gorgeous however there is no pump you can purchase a pump and then you could kind of reuse it if you plan on using this Foundation over and over and it also can be used for the Estee Lauder Double Wear foundation so I'm going to go ahead and get into the application I'll use a beauty blender on this side and then I'll use a more dense morphe brush on this side on the Mac website they describe this as a modern Foundation that combines a natural matte finish and medium to full buildable coverage with broad spec UVA UVB SPF 15 protection applies builds and blends easily and evenly comfortable in longwearing it helps to minimize the appearance of pores and Imperfections giving skin a smoother more Flawless look and finish it lists it as being longwearing for 8 hours refined skin comfortable all day oil control with oil-free sweat and humidity resistance for all skin types dermatologist tested and nonacne genic I've given the foundation a little bit of time to set and I think it's dried down to basically a powder finish so if you have a normal skin even combination skin you may be able to get away with not powdering if you are insanely oily like I am basically always have to powder I'm not going to use a setting spray I did not use a primer um while annoying anyways this show must go on I will put on the rest of my face now I feel like it built up to a gorgeous medium near full coverage I think I could build it up a little bit further I went in um with one light layer and then built it up some more and I think it built really nicely but I do think that you don't need to move really quickly but don't like let it completely set and build on top of that because it does dry down to a powder finish and I think it gave a really nice amount of coverage I liked both the beauty blender and the brush so I will put on the rest of my makeup and show you how it looks in photos I have the rest of my makeup on now and I'm wondering why it took me so long to jump on the bandwagon of this Foundation I'm loving it I will list everything else that I have on my face down below but I think that it just looks so good it doesn't look cakey on my skin it just gives beautiful coverage my liquidy cream concealer still went on well even though the foundation had set to a powder finish I lightly powdered and then everything else went on beautifully I think it looks so great on the skin in photos I'm also equally as impressed in um my kind of Studio lighting here it looks great as I always have been saying anyways the ring light makes most things look pretty good and then in flash photos however it surprised me CU I still think it looks amazing the SPF 15 doesn't seem to give really much of a flashback it looks great and natural and my skin just looks absolutely Flawless so I'm interested to see how this lasts throughout the day I hope it holds up and this is going to become like a staple in my collection for sure it is currently 10:48 a little bit of a later start to the day so I will check back with you guys at the end of it I don't know what it is about the 12 days of foundation but I have been having some amazing luck and coming across a ton of foundations that I've been loving this one seems to be no different I am super impressed with how this has worn I've had it on for about 8 n hours now and it looks a little bit shiny but nothing has moved around it's really just in my T-Zone that there is any shininess my transcript: Today is all about the new Make Up For Ever HD Skin Balancing and Perfecting Foundation. Look at this packaging. This foundation is $49 with lots and lots of claims. Long-wearing, tightens pores, it's supposed to have a true-to-tone match, last for 24 hours. There are 40 shades in this line, which is amazing. Apparently, this foundation is supposed to match to your skin's true tone without oxidizing or creating dullness. Supposed to have a natural finish, medium coverage. It has niacinamide, hyaluronic acid, cherry flower extract, and blueberry seed oil. That's a new one for me, which is supposed to comfort and calm the skin. So, we'll see. Either it's going to cover and calm my skin, or it's going to just break me out. Supposed to be lightweight, it's water-infused, and supposed to melt into your skin, be buildable, never cakey or heavy, which is true about Make Up For Ever foundations, honestly. I feel like they've really done well over the years not being cakey. This is also supposed to be waterproof and sweatproof. We will be checking in throughout the day, as well as tonight at the end of the night. First of all, let's talk about the packaging. Wonderful glass. I love the Make Up For Ever lip logo. I'm kind of obsessed with this. The curvature of the lid and the branding is so cute. I just overall really love this bottle compared to the HD Skin, which is just a classic foundation. You know, classic foundation bottle. Also, the pumps are amazing on their foundations, very controllable. You can do half a pump very easily. You never just accidentally have to like use a full pump if you don't want to, which I really, really love and appreciate about Make Up For Ever's pumps. This is the comparison in the HD Skin Balancing and Perfecting Foundation in shade 101 lace. And my shade in the Make Up For Ever HD Skin, which is Y108, which used to be Y225. And by the way, I'm not using a setting spray or a moisturizing spray or anything like that. I just have on my daily moisturizer, which is the Freck Rich moisturizer. I do feel like 101W Lace is a really good match for me. I applied my bronzer and my blush. For my bronzer, I used the Artist Color Crayon in Anywhere Caffeine for bronze. source.title: Maybelline Superstay Battle: Skin Tint or Lumi-Matte? (Review + All Day Wear Test)

transcript: Hello everyone, welcome. Today I'm going to be doing a comparison of the Maybelline SuperStay 24-hour Skin Tint and the Maybelline SuperStay 30hour Lumi Matte. Both are highly rated longwearing liquid foundations. I first tried the Lumi Matte a couple of months ago and I have been raving about it ever since. Prior to that, however, I would often rave about the Superstay 24-hour skin tint. I think this was the first skin tint that ever gave me the type of coverage that I was looking for. So many skin tints I've tried just have very very minimal coverage, which is fine if that's what you like, if that's what you feel confident in. But as someone who does have oily skin that does want a little bit more than just a light tint, I held off on trying this for a very long time because of my past experiences with skin tints not only being enough coverage, but also disappearing very quickly from my face due to the oils coming through and just sort of sucking up the product, the pigment. So, this foundation really checked off a lot of boxes for me. It had the coverage. It has the longevity. It contains vitamin C. And it's under $20. Once I started raving in my videos about this one, though, I was quite often asked to compare these two. This one claims to wear for 30 hours. This one claims 24. I personally don't wear my foundation for any longer than say 12 hours max. So, if you're looking for a 30hour wear test, unfortunately, I'm not going to give you that today. But I am going to wear it for at least 12 hours. So, when I come back at the end of this video, I'm probably going to look very different. I will most likely have my hair up in a messy bun and have a sweatshirt on because I'll likely be getting ready for bed soon. So, coming up, you're going to see an application of both foundations in the same shade, and then I'll do two check-ins throughout the day, and then the final check-in will be when I come back and let you see how everything is looking. Even though you're about to find out which side of my face has which one on it, I want you to take a guess right now at which side you think is which. So, I've already prepped my skin with the same serum and moisturizer on both sides. I'm going to put the Lumi Matte on the left side of my face, which is the side that has this little mole here. And then I'm going to use the SuperStay skin tint on the right side of my face. Keep in mind when I do my check-ins, I use the front camera of my iPhone, and that's going to flip the image. So, this mole on the left side of my face is going to appear on the right. So, I don't want that to confuse you when I do the check-ins because it has in the past. I've received comments from people that said, "You're pointing to the wrong side." So, once again, the Lumi Matte will be on the left and the skin tint will be on the right. And I am using the same shade 220 in both. I also have shade 120, which is probably what I should be using for this demo because I have not self-tanned recently, but I could not find my Maybelline skin tint in the lighter shade because I had 220 in both. That's what I decided to do this test with today. And I'll just drag it down my neck and chest. Let's shake up the Lumi matte. Going to pump some onto the back of my hand. And now with a foundation brush, I'm patting into the product and placing it all over one half of my face. And as you can see, it's definitely a little dark for me, but like I said, we're going to make it work. What I like about this is that a little bit goes such a long way. And it just looks so natural on the skin, I think, for a medium coverage foundation. I say medium coverage because I can still see some of my discoloration in this area coming through. And with a full coverage foundation, that is typically covered in one go. I don't have to layer any product. Okay, so I think we've got a line right down the middle. I have to say I am really loving this luminous matte category because even though I have oily skin and over the years I've always chosen a matte foundation. As we age, we also sort of lose a lot of our glow. While I don't want my skin to appear really oily, I also don't want it to be just flat and one-dimensional. I want my skin to look youthful. I think that's what we all want. So, I love that this gives me the best of both worlds. So, now I'm going to put the skin tint on using another BK Beauty brush, just the travel size version this time. And I'm going to put a few drops of this on my other hand. I did buy a pump on Amazon a while back that I put in my I think shade 120 of the skin tint. I'm not a big fan of the dropper and I really need to find that lighter shade. But anyway, let's dip in. I'm going to switch hands because I am left-handed and apply. So, this one is not nearly as spreadable as the Lumi Matte, and it's also quite a bit darker and warmer. I don't know if that's coming through on camera, but it's definitely coming through in my mirror. I'm going to try putting just some drops directly on the brush and seeing if that helps with the blendability. It's definitely more matte 100%. I think good thing I don't have anywhere to go today because I think that in this case people would be able to tell I was wearing two different foundations. When I've done videos like this in the past comparing different foundations, they've always been pretty close that I felt comfortable just walking around not thinking anybody would notice that I have two different foundations on. Once again, I think on camera it's not really showing as much of a different differential, but here in my studio lighting it is. All right. Now, I'm going to flip this mirror around. By the way, this is my IOS mirror that I always use every time I film. It's got a regular mirror on one side and then I think it's like a 10 times magnification on the other side. I actually have two of these mirrors. One here in my studio and one in my bedroom at my vanity. They are fantastic. Up close, the shades don't look as different. And the reason I say this side is more luminous is because I'm looking right at my forehead and I can see as I move my head back and forth, I see kind of like a sheen on this side. Not like a shimmery sheen, but like a glow on this side, but not on this side at all. I would say that the coverage is equal. I don't feel like the skin tint gave me any less coverage or any more coverage. My pores look exactly the same on both sides. Well, I take back what I said a few moments ago. I don't think anybody's going to be able to notice that I'm wearing two different foundations. So, now what I'm going to do is put the rest of my makeup on and then do some check-ins throughout the day and then come back tonight and let you know my thoughts. Which one is best for you? Let's find out. Here's what both sides are looking like after I applied the rest of my makeup. I did set with a little Laura Mercier Ultra Blur Powder. Okay, it's been about 4 hours and clearly I am shiny all over. This is the point where I would normally powder. Actually, I probably would have powdered already or at least blotted. On camera, they both look both sides look equally shiny. Again, here's my mold. So, this is the Lumi matte and this is the SuperStay skin tint. Coverage is the same on both. I haven't lost coverage either with either. Um, what I was saying is that right now it might look like I'm equally as shiny on both sides, but when I've looked in other mirrors around my house, the Lumi matte is definitely more lumy, more luminous than the skin tint. So, here's where we're at after about 4 hours, and I'll check in again with you in like another four. Okay, it's about 5:30 p.m. We are 8 hours into this wear test. I did powder down my face because I was quite shiny and I wanted to be able to show you guys how intact both sides still are as far as not losing coverage. And it's much easier to see without all of that shine being a distraction. So, here's what where we're where we're at. I don't know why on camera the top of my cheekbones like they look like they have some sort of discoloration. It's strange. Let me get more in the light here. Um, I'm trying really, really hard to see some major diff major differences between the two and I really can't. Again, the only little point of difference that I'm noticing, but I'm not sure anybody else would, is that to me, as I looked really, really close up in my mirror, the skin tint side, as I've been saying from the very beginning, is slightly more matte, which makes sense given that one is supposed to be more luminous. So, eight hours, folks. You really, really can't tell that I'm wearing two different colors, can you? Two different shades. All right, I will check back in maybe in another 4 hours. It could be 3. I don't know. Depends on how tired I am and how soon I want to go soon I want to get to bed. So, I'll try to do that last check-in about 4 hours from now around 9:30 p.m. back in my studio lighting cuz I this will do me no good because it'll be dark outside. I took one final look in my magnified mirror, and I truly cannot find much difference. With the exception of the vitamin C in this product, the skin tint, based on what I saw today, you could tell me that these were the exact same foundations, just in different packaging, and I would believe you. I know that's probably not the conclusion you were hoping I was going to come to, but I'm always going to be truthful and share my real experience. And this 12-hour wear test told me that both of these foundations are extremely longwearing. I honestly have not lost any coverage. Neither one is exceptionally smoothing or blurring, but they don't really claim to be. And with the added powder, that is where I was able to get the smoothness. And I guess I could argue that that's the reason why I don't reach for either of these when I do my going out or date night or event makeup. But if you are someone that works a 10-hour workday, a 12-hour workday, a 16, 18 hour workday, or you have an all day event to go to, either one of these would be a great choice just to have that coverage and keep that coverage all day long. And if you don't need any smoothing, if your skin is already in fabulous smooth condition, well, first of all, if that's the case, you are extremely blessed. And both of these foundations would likely check off all of the boxes for you. I'm going to close out this comparison by saying that if you were choosing between the two right now, I would take the packaging into consideration. Yes, you can buy a pump for this, which I mentioned earlier that I did do. You can buy a pump and put it in here if you don't like the dropper. This one already has a pump. Once again, this one contains vitamin C. This one does not. I did observe that the Lumi matte is slightly more luminous. I don't think I'm going to be able to wear this at all in the summertime or if I lived in a very humid climate as someone with oily skin. While this doesn't offer any oil control, I do feel like it is more matte. Even now, there's plenty of shine, but I feel like there's a little bit more on this side. So, I would love to know if any of you watching have done this comparison yourself and what your results were. I'm really, really curious. I guess at least now you know, if you've been using this and you've been loving it, you're really not missing anything by not picking this one up. or if you're using this one and you haven't tried the skin tint, you're not missing out on anything either because they truly are so so similar. If you have any questions about today's wear test, please do not hesitate to leave them for me in the comments. I do try to answer as many comments as I can. If you did enjoy this video, I really appreciate you giving it a thumbs up. And if you are not already subscribed to my channel, I hope that you will consider doing so. I do upload new videos at least twice per week. You can also find me on Instagram and Tik Tok and Facebook under the same username, Rhysa does makeup. Thank you so much for watching. I hope to see you in my next video. [Music]

source.title: my oily acne prone skin LOVES these foundations. here’s why you should try them too.

transcript: I have extremely oily, acne prone skin and for the longest time I've been looking for the best foundation in the market. So in today's video I'm going to be showing you guys some of my favorite foundations perfect for oily skinned acne people. The first foundation that I am recommending is a really natural foundation that is great for everyday wear. I would say it's good for any type of skin combination. I wear this during the winter time whenever my skin gets a little bit more dry, the summertime, springtime, anytime. I feel like this is a perfect all occasion foundation and it is the L'Oreal True Match. I am in the shade N4 neutral light medium. I really love the diversity and the range of colors in this collection. The only downside is that it can be a little bit overwhelming trying to find your perfect color. I really like it because the consistency is a runny formula, so it's easy to work with. If I could describe the texture on my skin and how it sits, I would say that it feels like a very nice matte film over my skin, I would say it almost feels like a second skin. It doesn't feel oily. It's not dewy, but it's also not super drying where it will cling on to your dry patches. But I really love this. If I could recommend a foundation for everyone, even if you're not super oily, then I would get this one. The second one that I will be showing you is a recent find. It is the Maybelline SuperStay Lumi Matte Foundation. Now, this is great for my girls out there who want to jump on the dewy skin bandwagon, but you have really oily skin. And we all know that dewy skin right now is a trend. And a lot of products on the market are going to be marketed as luminous, dewy. And as oily girls, we already know that those words are not going to look good on our skin. As an oily girl, I love it because it's still luminous but very matte. And at the end of the day, you just look so perfect. I'm also in shade 125. I'm about this much done. So, in like the next month or two, I'm going to have to get a new bottle. Last but not least, the foundation I'm going to show you is an oldie but a goodie. I feel like everyone talks about this. If you watch makeup tutorials or makeup reviews, a lot of people are going to recommend this foundation, and there's a reason why it's still everyone's favorite, and it is the L'Oreal Infallible Fresh Wear. I really love the coverage that this foundation gives me. Like I said, I have oily, acne prone skin, so that means I do have a lot of acne scarring. Um, I'm actually wearing this foundation today, but I don't ever really need to go in with concealer on my acne scars. One coat of this is more than enough to cover everything, which I really appreciate. I would say this is a pretty comfortable foundation to wear every single day. The only downside is that it is a little bit thicker. I hope you guys enjoyed my top three foundations for oily skin. It's taken me a really long time to find foundations that work for my skin type. So, if you do decide to give any of these a try, please let me know down below which one is your favorite. analysis.product_reviewed: NARS Soft Matte Complete Foundation
analysis.key_quote: It feels very comfortable, definitely lightweight, doesn't feel heavy or thick, and after about 11 hours with no touch ups, it looks okay with a nice glowy look, not oily or messy.

source.title: The ONLY foundation tutorial you NEED: A Pro's guide to flawless looking skin (all skin types)

transcript: Stop wasting your money on foundations that never look right. I guarantee you it's not the foundation you're buying. It's the techniques that you're using to get the look that you want. I want to show you all the steps that you need to know for the various skin types. Dry, combination, oily. Now, before we dive into specific skin types, there's some general principles that everyone needs to know about foundation. Number one, skin prep is paramount. If you aren't prepping your skin, doing exfoliation a couple days a week, cleansing it properly, adding a good moisturizer, maybe a serum if you need it, if your skin care routine isn't tight and giving you that smooth skin, it doesn't matter what foundation you put on top. If your skin underneath is dry and flaky or it's super greasy and oily, start with your skincare routine because that's the basis for everything. Number two, you need to find your perfect shade and undertone. And I'll put a video here and I'll link to it below on undertones. I did a whole video, woo, whole video on that on if you're neutral, cool, or warm. If you don't know, check that out. And then the last thing you need are different tools of the trade. I like to have a few different tools. I like a good buffing brush like this. This is the Nissa Pinnacle foundation brush. I talk about all the time. It's great for buffing into the skin. I also like something a bit softer and angled like this. Um, this is the BK 101. If I want something a bit softer and a bit more diffused, and then you've got to have a good sponge. These are the paw pot sponges from Amazon. It's like $10 for a whole bag of these suckers. that's going to give you the results if you want to tap your foundation on. Now, let's start first with dry skin. So, your main goals with dry skin is going to be hydration. It's preventing flakiness. It's avoiding that cakey look and it's preventing your foundation from clinging to those dry patches on your skin. Now, for dry skin in the morning, my skincare routine, I keep it very simple. Gentle cleanser. Don't use anything too harsh at all. You don't want to strip the skin. And then apply vitamin C serum. I use this every single morning. You all, please, I'm going to get close to the camera. If you're not using vitamin C serum every morning, it helps with hyperpigmentation. is great antioxidants for your skin. Just just use it. Trust me, your skin will thank you. And then if you're really really dry, I like to add in an essence just for extra moisture. I love this one by Haruharu. I talk about it all the time because it's very light and milky. And then on top, you can use a moisturizer. Make sure it's nice and rich, but nothing too greasy or heavy. And then the last step is the super important one, and is an SPF. I like to choose an SPF with luminosity. I really love this E.L.F. Cosmetics one because it has some luminosity to it. It's glowing. It really makes my foundation look beautiful on top. I'm telling you, if you have dry skin, using a luminous SPF is game changer for giving you that really glowing skin that dry skin actually needs. Now, whenever it comes to dry skin, I really like prepping the skin. That's the most important part with something very hydrating. I love the E.L.F. Thirst Burst Holy Hydration Drops because it's almost like a lightweight gel type formula. I'm going to use this kind of as a primer just to add extra hydration to the skin. Now, the trick with dry skin is to choose a formula obviously that is a dewy finish foundation, but you want to look for something that's a thin viscosity. Put a drop on the back of your hand, tilt it up. If you see that dripping down your hand, that's a thin viscosity. Pair it with a brush. So, I'm taking the Ana Pinnacle foundation brush, one drop of foundation, and I start buffing this into the skin. I want it to blend flawlessly over that hydrating E.L.F. primer that I just put down. And the formulas you want to look for for dry skin are thin viscosity, dewy finish, high pigmentation. The more pigmentation you have in a foundation, the less product that you want to use. That's the most critical thing. Please, if you get anything from this video, if you have dry skin, the less product you can use, the better. The more layers you have to put a foundation on, instantly it's going to stick to those dry patches and make you look cakey and thick. Now, for powder for dry skin, use sparingly or nothing at all. even describe it it's such an interesting formula of this foundation it definitely doesn't feel heavy or fake on the skin it just gives you a better full coverage but you can still have this nice skin foundations that i have they are one fluid outs or 30 mils and they are around similar price this one is much bigger and it's just the same price which was amazing the actual bottle is not glass it's
