source.title: RARE BEAUTY Foundation and Concealer | REVIEW + 8 hour wear test on OILY skin!

transcript: Hi everyone. Welcome back to my channel. Today I'm trying out the new foundation and concealer from Rare Beauty, which is Selena Gomez's new line and I am so excited to try these. I did purchase these with my own money and I wanted to try it out on camera so we see how the application process goes and I also will check in later to show you how these products wear throughout my day. Let's just start with the packaging. I think the packaging is really sleek. I like the gold writing of Rare Beauty on the front and I really appreciate this top. There's a ball on the top of several of the products in the line and that is actually to help those with arthritis to open packaging easier. So I love that this is inclusive packaging. And I bought the color 170W which on the website said it was for light warm olive skin tones and if that is actually true and this is a good color match, I will be so excited because I do have a light warm olive undertone and if you haven't watched my video on how to find your undertone, I will link it above. But if this foundation works, it will be one of the very first foundations that will match my skin tone perfectly. So I'm crossing my fingers that it's a good match. For the concealer, I bought the shade 130N because I normally go lighter with concealer under my eyes. The one thing I do like about this line though is that the foundation and the concealers match. So if I wanted to get the same exact color concealer as the foundation 170W, I could have done that and depending on how this wears, I might go back and get that shade to match this. So let's start with the foundation. And it has a really big doe foot applicator. And you are meant to just put it on your face in dots. And the website says if you want lighter coverage, you should just put four dots around the face and blend it out. If you want medium to fuller coverage, you want to put four swipes of the foundation around. I'm going to start with just lighter coverage and see how that goes. On this side of my face, I'm going to use a brush and on this side of my face, I will use my beauty blender and we'll just see what applicator works best with this foundation. So just a few dots. And I'm going to use the Real Techniques Expert face brush on this side of my face. That is a really good color match. I like that. Okay, beauty blender on this side. So I can't really tell much of a difference between the sides. Obviously four dots is not enough for my face because I haven't been able to cover anything around the nose and I'm missing spots around here. But I think the brush makes the product go longer. I was able to get more product around this part of my face than with the beauty blender on this side. So I think a brush application works really nicely. I am going to add more coverage so that I can get more coverage over all of these red spots, especially on this center of my face. I'm going to stick with the same uh applicators on both sides. Okay, after I layered more product, first off, this is an amazing match. If you have light warm olive skin, I think this might be the best foundation match I have ever tried and that is amazing. When I put a second coat of foundation on this side with my beauty blender, it actually gives me fuller coverage. So I actually like this. On this side, it looks a bit more light coverage, but it looks really natural. I do have some pigmentation poking through. I don't know if you can see it through the camera, but there's some redness here. Um but I like it. Um it feels a little bit tacky on this side. But honestly, it's a pretty nice dry down. It's not very oily. It doesn't feel like it's going to move around throughout the day. I like that. So I'm going to put a little bit more coverage with the beauty blender on this side over my pigmentation and my nose is not actually holding on to any color, which is pretty normal for me. My nose has a hard time taking pigment from foundations. Um and put a little bit more around here with the beauty blender. And I will bring it down just a little bit onto my neck just to make sure there's no harsh line here even though because we have such a good color match, I almost don't need to do that. But let's put a bit more coverage on. And there we have basically as much full coverage as I think we can get. I really like how it's layering. Like I put more around here and as you saw me, I kept putting more here as well. And to be honest, it still looks really natural. Again, there's still pigmentation coming through, but it looks like a really natural foundation and if you really want to have really stunning looking skin, very natural looking skin, it's actually better to use a lighter coverage foundation and then just spot conceal where you need it. So I kind of wish that I got the 170W in the concealer as well because that would have been really nice to use the same color to spot conceal. But that's okay. I'll just use normal concealers to do that later on. So let's move to concealer. Again, I purchased the shade 130N which is lighter than the foundation. And I'm going to again use a brush on this side and the beauty blender on this side. It's a very interesting applicator shape. I don't know if I've ever seen an applicator like this, but it has a triangular shape which I think it's going to be really nice to kind of hug the nose area. I'm not going to put too much on to begin with just cuz again, I want to see how far this goes. But let's just start with that. I'm going to use another Real Techniques brush and this is 300. That looks really natural. I like that. And then beauty blender on the other side. Both sides look really good. I do think again, when you lay down the first amount of concealer or foundation, the brush spreads the product farther, but you get more natural coverage at least with the concealer, I think with the beauty blender. But I mean, I like them both. That's really nice and I think my skin looks like skin. It doesn't look really overly made up. It doesn't look cakey. I'm pretty impressed. I'm going to go and do the rest of my makeup and then I will check back in after that to see how things are settling in and then I will also check in at the end of the day. So here's the finished look with the rest of my makeup on. It's about an hour and a half later. I did my makeup and then I did a few other things. So I do have some thoughts to share about the foundation and the concealer. So let's start with the foundation. I didn't set the foundation right away after I finished putting it on and I noticed that it settled into my fine lines here on my forehead. That happens with most foundations nowadays, but this also went all the way across cuz obviously when I move my face, there's a lot of wrinkling up there. So I didn't really like that. However, I did then go ahead and set it with my Kat Von D powder and I kind of bake when I do that. So I put the powder on and I just leave it there as if I were baking under my eyes. And that usually helps things to stop from settling in those lines. So now that I've powdered there and it's been settling for a few hours, it looks quite good. The same thing happened under here with my under eye concealer, especially on this side. I do have a lot of fine lines under there. And I'll see if I can get a little bit closer to show you, but there are a lot of creases under there. Again, that's something that happens with nearly all of my concealers at the moment just because I have fine lines. Now, one thing I'll say is normally I powder quite a bit under here to make sure that things are locked into place. I did a really light dusting of powder today. So, if you use a bit more powder, maybe it will not crease. But, I really love how the rest of the foundation looks. It has not settled into my fine lines here, which I really like. On the rest of my face, I actually didn't powder here at all, and it's holding up really nicely. I'm actually really, really impressed. This is definitely not a full coverage foundation for me, but I think the serum quality of this foundation, it's definitely not a thick foundation. I think it has a lot of pigment, and I didn't feel like I had to use a ton of product to get the coverage that I currently have. This is not full coverage at least on me, and now that it's sat on my face for a little bit, I am seeing a lot more of my pigmentation under my eyes coming through. I don't really like that, but um you know, it is what it is. So, I actually think I want to try just with my fingers putting a little bit more on. I have powdered already, so this could be a big mistake, but let's just see what happens. It's quite a bit. Um let me take my concealer brush actually, and I'm going to put it on both sides. That actually covers really well, and it smooths out all of the wrinkles. So, I said I would get closer so you could see, but now the wrinkles don't exist anymore. Okay, I like that. So, if you get creasing with this particular product, and if you have already powdered, you can layer it on and a little tiny bit of product, and it gives me back that coverage. I like that about this. I think that's really helpful for touch-ups. Normally, I don't touch up my concealer throughout the day, but the fact that I can use maybe a lighter coverage, and then refresh it throughout the day is a really nice feature of this. So, that's really it right now. I'm going to continue wearing the foundation and concealer, and I will check back in at the end of the day with my final thoughts. So, I wanted to get a little bit closer to the camera, so hopefully you can see what I'm talking about. So, if I move even closer, so there's a bit of a line right across here, and I unfortunately can't get any closer to my camera for it to get in focus. But, hopefully you can see a little bit now. You have to be really close to my face to see that. So, I don't think that creasing is really horrible. And then underneath my eyes, you can see there's a little bit of black right here, and I'm not sure where that came from. It's not on this side, but obviously the concealer has moved there and come off. And then if you can see on this side, there's a tiny bit of creasing right in here, which is pretty typical for my foundation to do and my concealer just because I do have fine lines there. If I just move my face around here though, I think you can see that it looks really nice in all of the rest of the areas. There's no creasing here. There's no creasing on my chin. It just looks really nice, and I'm pretty pleased with it. I think the foundation has worn really well. I think it still looks fresh and natural. I do have a bit more oiliness peaking through now, but after 8 hours, that's pretty typical. I don't think it looks like I'm an oil slick though. I think it looks actually dewy and very healthy, which is a testament to the foundation because sometimes in other foundations, if I start to get oily throughout the day, the foundation will break apart, and I will look incredibly oily. So, I think this is wearing really well despite my oily skin. I think this is a win in my book. I will say normally I'm a full coverage type of girl. I like everything to be canceled out, but this is not full coverage, and I'm really loving it. So, if you want something that has buildable coverage that doesn't look like a mask, it looks really natural and wears relatively well throughout the day, I think you should give this foundation a try. And again for the concealer, not full coverage, does crease a little bit, but if you want something that's really natural for those no makeup makeup days, I think this is a really nice product. So, I hope that was helpful. I am curious about more products in the line, so I think I will be purchasing more. So, look out for a video with some of the other products from Rare Beauty. I also have heard that more products will be launching soon in addition to everything they launched in this first iteration. So, I am looking forward to what Selena Gomez and her team are going to create next. If you've tried any of these products and you like them, or things worked or didn't work for you, let me know in the comments below. I'd love to know what your thoughts are. And of course, if you like this video, please go ahead and give it a thumbs up, and go ahead and subscribe to my channel. I would really, really appreciate it if you do. I hope you're having a wonderful day wherever you are, and I'll see you next time.

source.title: Revlon Colorstay Foundation Review (Oily Skin) 12 Days of Foundation Day 5

transcript: hi guys welcome back to the 12 days of foundation today is day number five and we're going to be testing out a very iconic Foundation next to like Estee Lauder Double Wear I feel like this was at least one of the foundations you heard the most about and that is the redlon coloray foundation I'm going to be using mine in combination to oily because of course I have very oily skin and we're going to be putting it to the test throughout the day I will link to the rest of the 12 days of foundation down below so let's get started this Foundation has been around for a very long time I remember when I first started watching YouTube this was probably one of the things I went out and bought first because so many people talked about it not only does it come in a combination oily formula but there's also a normal to dry which I love it's being even more inclusive there's about 15 Shades in the range on the Revlon website however it listed like six but on the Ulta website and some other drugstore website it lists a much larger range I've always had a hard time finding toast here in Canada which is the shade that I'm wearing 372 toast about an nc42 nc43 for reference so let me know down below what kind of shade range um is where you live cuz I know that they kind of mix it up depending on where in the country you are you're in Canada United States what state what province that kind of thing retails for about $12 to $18 definitely a little bit on the higher price for a drugstore foundation and recently they came out with a pump that is not the one I have I bought this a few months ago um waiting to do a review on it and I'm just kind of put it off to the side but it does come with a pump now which is awesome and it comes in a glass bottle which makes it a little bit easier to see what you've got in there you get a full fluid ounce of product as well so let's get into the application that is my cat not a baby I'm going to be using a beauty blender on one side and a um e.l.f ultimate blending brush on the other this Foundation has been reformulated several times put in new a new bottle recently with the pump the formula that I have is the one just before the pump so it doesn't list having an SPF but online here it says it's a foundation that Smooths on evenly for a Flawless look Color Stay makeup with soft Flex SPF for combination oily skin by Revlon features soft Flex technology that will not rub off with normal wear oil-free formula looks fresh feels natural and controls excess oil it also recommends that you blend it in quickly I had no problems blending it out doing one half of my face and then the other but if you move really slowly you might want to keep that in mind I've given the foundation a few minutes to set I did my brows while I was waiting I think it gives a really great amount of coverage I built up about two layers very close to full so if you have anything you you're looking to cover and you want a good drugstore foundation I think that this is a great option I don't think that it's dyed down completely matte but it is starting to set on my skin I'm going to use a powder either way I don't feel like it highlights any kind of texture or pores in my skin nothing more than normal I don't really have major issues with that but if you do you can of course get the help of a por filling primer so I'm going to put on the rest of my makeup oh for application I liked both sides Beauty blender is generally faster for me so I'd probably go that route but I'm going to put on the rest of my makeup and show you how it looks in photographs everything went on really smoothly while I was putting on the rest of my makeup I had no problem blending in my liquid kind of cream concealer even though the foundation had kind of set it looks super super Flawless on the skin I would definitely say this is nearing full coverage depending on what kind of skin issues you have you may always need to kind of color correct or do some spot concealing depending on what you've got going on but for me it definitely gives like just as much coverage as I would ever need it does not look super cakey on the skin or cakey at all really um but I would be careful in application I can see how this could go a little bit too far um but I love the way it looks in photos of course it's going to look great when you've got a nice full coverage Foundation like this and then in um Flash phot I didn't really have any issues with flashback I think it looks super natural there's a Sheen to the skin it doesn't look matte or caked on and I don't feel like um it would cling on to any kind of acne or anything like that but if you do of course have a dryer skin type with dry patches then I would recommend going for the normal to dry formula so so far so good we're going to see how this lasts throughout the day if it oxidizes if it moves around if everything else stays on my face but so far so great and I will check back with you at the end of the day so so it looks like we have another winner it is now the end of the day and I am super impressed with how this wore I think it wore beautifully I'll beat them a little bit shiny but that is just basically to be expected it did not wear off on my face it did not oxidize it doesn't feel greasy it even kind of has that little bit of a tight feeling um you know when you're wearing something a little bit matte and it just kind of feels tight on your face that may sound negative but like when you have oily skin that's kind of dreamy like you kind of want that um the oils started to come th through around 12 or 1 which is pretty normal for me I'm just going to blot so I can show you how it would have reacted if I had blotted throughout the day and um I'm just really loving this I'm glad to have it back in my life it's been years since I've tried this and I think the fact that it is super hyped up is totally valid like there's a reason for it I was actually going to blot then powder but I don't even think I need to do that I think it looks amazing now that I have blotted this is going to be an absolute favorite of mine it's going to come back into like the daily rotation the only negative about this is that it could potentially get cakey but that's up to you um I would recommend a beauty blender go in with really light layers until you achieve the coverage and look that you want but overall huge thumbs up absolutely impressed and highly recommend I will link to the rest of the 12 days of foundation down below along with the rest of my oily skin Diaries series and if you'd like to connect with me you can find me on Instagram Twitter and Snapchat @ Samantha Jan YT and I'll see you guys next time bye

analysis.product_reviewed: Revlon Colorstay Foundation Combination to Oily

source.title: 5 FOUNDATIONS I HATE | Oily & Acne Prone Skin

transcript: hey guys welcome back recently I did an updated top five foundations that will be linked Down Below in case you missed it where I talked about all of my current favorite foundations and then I was thinking I should do a video on foundations I don't like so this is going to be my bottom five a few of these you guys have seen me talk about before in First Impressions and whatnot but then I also wanted to include some ones I haven't talked about on camera or maybe haven't talked about in a while because I figured this would be just as helpful and I think all of these foundations I did use one of my skin was still very oily all but one of them actually I just realized but if you have oily and acne prone skin then this might be very helpful for what foundations you should avoid so let's get started Foundation number one is the Revlon color State I tried like the combination to oily or whatever that version is and I actually loved this foundation in general because it had really full coverage it was so transfer proof so longwearing but I ended up having an allergic reaction to it I broke out in a rash there was like tiny little irritated red bumps in like little clusters all over my face and it did kind of end up breaking me out in general as well and this was like the only new thing I had introduced to my makeup routine and as soon as I stopped using it the rash went away after like a day so I don't use Revlon foundations because of this I won't ever again just because I don't want to risk it I have never actually heard of anyone else having an allergic reaction to it and I do have like ridiculously sensitive skin so this is one that like you could still try out if you want to cuz it's not likely you'll have the same experience as me and overall it is a good foundation number two is the good old Becca aqual luminous I'm sure most of you guys have seen my first impressions on this I did try this out once my skin was like perfectly balanced out from Accutane wasn't oily it wasn't dry it was just like right in between and this was just such a disaster on me you guys even not being oily this was so so greasy and just like wet looking it applied streaky it looked like thick and Cakey and gross on my skin it accentuated every little bit of texture every little bump every little line every pore and on top of all of that it made me like full on Oompa Loompa orange like the worst oxidation I have ever seen in my life it was awful people commented on that video getting mad at me for not setting it with powder and the only reason I didn't was because I knew it was like coming off immediately but the thing is when you have a foundation that's that like shiny and like gross already if you're going to put powder on top of it first of all I would have had to put on so much powder to actually mat y it my face would have actually turned into cake batter like it would have just been thick and disgusting it would have got like goopy and like clumpy and people also commented we're like oh my God it's not that bad like stop complaining just like powder it and it'll be fine but if I hadn't had all of those other issues with it the oxidation alone still makes it completely unwearable so this is definitely the worst Foundation I have ever tried I know some people absolutely love it so no offense to any of you guys if you do it just was not for me I had too many issues with it number three is the bare mineral bare skin Foundation I got this in gratus at Sephora because it launched when I was working there and this is mostly just a foundation that isn't for me it's definitely meant for more Flawless skin that wants light coverage and has less imperfections than I do but at the same time we got so so many returns at Sephora on this Foundation it was really hyped up too when it came out like Bare Minerals did a bunch of commercials for it and it's really just a foundation that's only going to work for a small group of people so I think that was kind of a mistake on their part honestly it's just very like thin and runny and sheer because it's so thin if you have like pores or anything like that it really will sink into them and accentuate them and it just looks kind of like greasy at the same time and kind of like smears around like it really didn't offer any kind of coverage for me personally and it was just kind of a mess honestly like I tried applying it a few different times and I would go to start applying it and I'd be like nope I can't do this and like remove it second to last we have got the Maybelline Dream velvet I was really disappointed in this one because it gave such amazing coverage at first but this has such high silicone content that as soon as you go to apply anything over top of it it just immediately starts like slipping off and sliding around and it disappears which is the last thing you want when you have like acne or scarring or anything like that because it just completely undoes all of the work you did covering up any imperfections if you're going to try this Foundation forget wearing bronzer blush Contour even brow products because nothing applies over top of this nicely and that's having already set it with a powder all over whether you use like a mattifying translucent powder a powder foundation it just made me look like a blotchy melting mess and the funny thing is I know some people didn't watch that entire review because at first I really really liked it but then like 2 seconds later once I started applying my face products I hated it and I said that in the video and so people commented and were like oh my gosh this looks great I'm totally going to go buy it thanks for the review and I was like you clearly did not watch the entire video and finally we have got the makeup forever matte velvet plus Foundation this is probably going to be pretty shocking to some of you guys because you think matte oily skin it would be great but from what I've gathered from my own experience and other people's reviews is that this is more for people who have like normal to like maybe a little bit combination skin that still want a matte finish this in my personal experience does not do well on actually very oily skin I'm sure it works well for some oily skinn people but what it did on me was it separated so so badly and it wasn't like normal Foundation separation where you just get kind of like greasy and it kind of like maybe patches off a little bit this made my skin look so dry and dehydrated what it looked like basically was it looked like I had taken a foundation and applied it over like really dry peeling cracked dead skin and my skin was so oily at this point I did not have like a stitch of dryness on my skin but it made my skin looks so Ultra dry it was really weird honestly in all the areas where my skin would typically get oily like around my nose around my mouth and like in between my brows it looked like I had like thick dead dry skin on my skin which was so bizarre and I was working at Sephora when I tried it and I remember like showing the people that I worked with I was like look what this foundation's doing on me and they were like ew no no like that is not cute I've never had that happen with any other Foundation it was just the weirdest thing ever and I've heard other people with oily skin have had the same experience but then people I talk to who have normal skin and like little to no imperfections know it looks really nice on them so it just kind of depends I guess but definitely not for me those were my five least favorite foundations that I have tried I hope you guys find this video helpful and found it interesting I talk a lot about the foundations I do like so I thought talking about the ones I don't would be kind of interesting and a little bit fun if there are any foundations that you have tried that didn't work for you feel free to share them in the comments down below but give this video a thumbs up if you did find it helpful go follow me on Twitter Instagram and Snapchat I am @ Siri Ranna on three and hit that subscribe button down below if you like this video and want to stick around for more but I will talk to you guys in the next one bye guys

analysis.product_reviewed: Revlon Colorstay 24 Hours Foundation for Combination to Oily Skin
analysis.key_quote: It is a lightweight, medium coverage foundation that lasted me about 12 hours, controlled oil mostly but not fully, did not cause breakouts, and is not full coverage so it didn’t build up cakey.

source.title: Foundation Friday Over 50 #26 Revlon ColorStay

transcript: [Music] Hey everybody, it's Angie and welcome to another Foundation Friday for over 50s. Yep, we're still doing Foundation Friday here because we have yet to find more than one holy grail and a handful of foundations that really work well for older, more mature, less than perfect skin. Today, we're going to take a look at Revlon Coloray Makeup. Now, the reason I have two is because this comes in two different formulations. This one is for uh combo to oily skin and this one is for normal to dry skin. I've heard that they've both been reformulated this year. I believe the reformulation is really just that they both contain sunscreen now and it's a all mineral sunscreen. So, they put in some titanium dioxide and some zinc oxide, which are my favorite kind of sunscreens anyway that don't irritate my skin. But it's funny, they gave this one the um combo to oily, an SPF of 15, and they give this one the normal to dry and SPF of 20. Why they wouldn't put in the same in both, I don't get. According to Revlon, this is a flawless matte medium to full coverage foundation. It's oilfree and it contains salicylic acid to help balance skin. The normal to dry formula is a flawless demi matte coverage that lasts up to 24 hours. It is also oil free, but it contains hyaluronic acid to hydrate skin over time. They're both basically silicone and water uh mixtures. They each have a couple of plant extracts in there. And even though it doesn't list fragrance as a separate uh ingredient on the label, they both are fragranced. I could smell both of them. This one seemed to be more fragrant to me than the uh dry to normal one. And this one also does contain some SD alcohol. The combo to oily skin formula comes in 22 different shades while the normal to dry only comes in 13 for us older people. Here are the swatches on these. The one for combo to oily skin is in 300 golden beige and the one for normal to dry skin is in 250 fresh beige. They always came in a nice heavyweight glass bottle, but now they all come with a pump dispenser which is awesome. I hate the ones where you have to pour it out. The first day that I used it, I used the combo to oily formula. I had used a chemical sunscreen. I put it on with my fingers with no primer and no setting powder. The application was slightly smeary with the fingers and a little uneven, but overall I felt like it looked pretty nice, especially from a distance. One pump gave sheer to medium coverage that definitely needed concealer, but I liked how it looked. It didn't settle into pores or make them look bigger. This gave a soft satin finish that even though it's supposed to be full-on matte, it really wasn't a full matte. But it did make my chin immediately like break out in these itchy, blotchy hives that didn't really improve throughout the day. I felt like it was definitely a nice looking foundation, especially from a distance. Up close, it looked just fine. It didn't really do anything to smooth over my pores or wrinkles, but it didn't settle into them either. at the 5h hour check-in. Um, I came back and from a distance I was like, "Wow, this stuff is awesome. Look at the wear on this." I've tried so many foundations that I come back at 5 hours and they are completely worn off or my nose looks like Rudolph the Red-Nosed Reindeer. And this one with no primer, it was barely worn off at all. So, I was really, really happy with it. It wasn't really getting too shiny. It was a little bit more glowy in my T-zone. It didn't look overly shiny. It didn't make me want to blot or powder, but unfortunately there was a little micro cracking in some areas, especially on the lower part of my face. So, I usually come back for a 10-hour check-in, but that day I was late. And so, this is after 11 hours of wear. It's worn off a little bit more and getting a little bit patchy, but overall from a distance, it still looks pretty good. for the entire 11 hours. It didn't settle into my pores and it didn't settle into wrinkles with the exception of the breaking out in hives on the lower part of my chin and kind of breaking up on the surface a little bit looking a little cracked down here. It really did have great wear and everything. So, I was psyched about it. I thought, "Oh, this is going to be an easy fix. I'll use a moisturizing primer and it will solve all the problems and it will in fact be the holy grail from the drugstore." Well, oh contr Pierre, was I ever wrong. I used a new sunscreen that I was testing. It was the Dr. Wild for Origins Mineral Sunscreen. I really like this sunscreen on its own, but I hate it under makeup. On top of that, I used the NYX Hydro Touch Primer to add a little bit of moisture back into my skin and applied with a blending sponge. That heavy shiny base just was not a good foundation for the foundation. It sat right on top and it looked really masky. definitely gave me fuller coverage, but it also settled right into wrinkles. And unfortunately, the addition of those other products didn't keep it from being drying around my chin. It just was not comfortable to wear. I did like it a lot from, you know, here up. So, I decided, even though I had reviewed it already, that I would go out and pick up the normal to dry formula. I gave this one a try on its own on my whole face. I used the hydropeptide sunscreen underneath. I gave it a spritz with the BA white charcoal mattifying makeup setting spray and used a sponge and it went on much much better than it did over the other mineral sunscreen. So, from a distance it looked really good. It looked smooth and it had a nice skin-like finish and it was very flattering. From up close, you could see that on the surface it was slightly broken up, but overall it was much much more comfortable to wear. At the five-hour check-in, I was impressed again at how longwearing this was. It still looked really good from up close and from a distance. It was only a little bit worn off around the edges of my nose, and it hadn't been drying and itchy to wear. So, for me, it was a vast improvement over the formula that's for uh combo to oily skin. And at the 10-hour check-in, it looked really good still. It was more worn off than the other formula was at this point in the day, but other than that, I thought it still looked really good for 10 hours. I did try this one on a fifth day with yet another sunscreen. Uh, again, a mineral sunscreen, but that I thought it might play well with. It didn't. I tried to apply it with two different brushes just to see how they went on with a brush. Um, the brush really left it kind of patchy and smeary. Today, I have them both on together. So, I have this one on the lower half of my face and I have this one on the upper half of my face. So, in order to help it to work successfully today for the video, I went back to the ISDIN chemical sunscreen. And on top of that, I used a spritz of the Smashbox primer water, and that seemed to work pretty well as far as setting it up with a nice thin surface layer to adhere to, and the makeup went on much smoother. I did use the sponge again today. And let's go ahead and do the phone test. So, here is my sparkling clean phone glass. not sparkling and clean anymore. Wow. And of course, the flash photography test. So, my takeaway from both of these is that while they can be uh really good, long lasting foundations with a really nice finish that look really good from a distance, it all depends on what your kind of skincare routine is that you use underneath it. I had great success with both of them when I used a chemical sunscreen underneath, which is what most of the people on the planet use. I know a lot of people don't even wear sunscreen every day. Even though I'm always telling you to do it, you don't listen to me. No. If I could only be there to put it on you in the morning, I would. But unfortunately, it just did not work well with thicker sunscreens or with thicker primers. If you're looking for a longwearing uh semi matte foundation from the drugstore, these could really fit the bill. I feel like these are very, very good. They're going kind of towards the top of the list. If you'd like to see where this ranks on the list of all the foundations that I have ever tested, I'll put the link to the list in the information box below the video, as well as a link to my video on my top five picks for the best drugstore foundations. I'll also put the link to that right up here so you can click over to that video. And I'll include the link for my top five high-end foundations as well because that is where my holy grail foundation lives. It is the Chanel Perfection Lumiere Velvet. This fulfills all of my wishes and dreams, all my wildest dreams for a foundation for mature skin. And so the search continues for another holy grail. Sadly, it was not the Revlon Coloray products, but they're not bad. So, I hope you found the video helpful and informative. I thank you for your time as always. So, take care everybody and have a great day. Bye-bye.

analysis.product_reviewed: Revlon ColorStay Foundation combo to oily formula in 300 golden beige and normal to dry formula in 250 fresh beige

persona.shade_used: 300 golden beige (combo to oily), 250 fresh beige (normal to dry)

analysis.key_quote: From a distance I was like, "Wow, this stuff is awesome. Look at the wear on this." ... At the 5 hour check-in it was barely worn off at all... but it did make my chin immediately like break out in these itchy, blotchy hives that didn’t really improve throughout the day.

source.title: Testing e.l.f. Flawless Satin Foundation!

transcript: Today on Foundation Lady, I'm going to be doing a fast foundation review on a very affordable foundation. It's the Elf Flawless Satin Foundation, and I'm going to be mixing shades 120 Pearl with 220 Beige. Let's get started. Pre-foundation application, I started out with the Elf Poreless Putty Primer, which a lot of people say is a dupe for the Tatcha Silk Canvas Primer. Absolutely agree. We'll talk more about that later. Then I went in with the Benefit Boi-ing Concealer in shade one on my red spots. Starting on the left side of my face with the shades Pearl and Beige mixed together, and it looks too light for my skin once I first start, but once it oxidizes, it's going to be the perfect shade. And speaking of oxidation, let me show you what both of these foundations look like, both freshly applied and dried. On top we have shade 120 Pearl, and on bottom 220 Beige. Elf describes this foundation as a lightweight foundation that blends into the skin naturally and hydrates using glycerin. Now, they claim that it's good for all skin types, which we'll talk a little bit about later. It's buildable, and it has a semi-matte finish. It comes in 40 inclusive shades. You get 0.68 oz or 20 mil. And normally, this foundation costs $6, but right now it's on sale for $4.80 on the Elf website. I just got it at Walmart for 480. I think on Amazon, it's even cheaper than that, so super, super affordable. I'd also like to mention that this is vegan and cruelty-free, in addition to being free of all those things that are really hard to pronounce: phthalates, parabens, hydroquinone. I'll put up a screenshot here of the other words. And finally, as far as the basics are concerned, I should mention that this does have fragrance in it, and there's no SPF. As far as application method goes, beauty sponge or fingers was my favorite method of application. I may be overly cautious about this, but I try not to pull at my skin, and I found when using the brush with this, I just had to pull a little bit more than I was comfortable with to try to get it to blend out the way that I wanted it to. I mentioned earlier that Elf says this is for all skin types. I definitely think all skin types can make this work. However, I think it's probably best for oily, combination oily, normal skin, and even slightly dry like I have. I think if you have dry to very dry skin, and you want to stay within the Elf family, your best bet would be to try the Elf Camo CC Cream. Now, it is more expensive than this foundation, but you get a lot more product, and I personally think it is one of Elf's best products of all time. It's absolutely beautiful. It's very creamy. There's a lot of shade options. It's honestly better than a lot of high-end CC creams I've tried. So, after I put on all of my makeup, my impression was, "Oh, wow, this is a good foundation. It's very no-nonsense, no-frills. It looks nice on the skin." Throughout the day, as I caught glimpses of my skin in the mirror, it looked better and better. Something happened with it just adjusting to my skin or even just a little bit of natural oil coming through, and it just looked prettier and prettier as the day wore on. I definitely think that the combination of the Elf Putty Primer with this foundation definitely helped. And this not only lasted a long time, but it looked really good the entire time. Here we are at the 7-hour mark. Here we are at the 10-hour mark while I'm sitting on the couch eating Cheetos and watching The White Queen. I mean, this was really really This really impressed me. And I'm just kind of laughing to myself because, you know, it was just a few weeks ago I tried a 60-something dollar foundation that didn't last as long as this, that didn't look as good as this. And it just goes to show that you can find wonderful products at the drugstore. I hope this helps you out. Have a beautiful day, and I'll see you soon.
